_rationale: C++ no-mode get_context is id-only, matching Go's handleGetObjectContext (internal/mcp/handlers.go), which builds its objectIDs list solely from args.ID and never resolves args.Name in the default no-mode path. A name-only call passes validateGetContextParams (id OR name is required, not both) but resolves nothing, so both binaries return {"contexts":[],"count":0} for get_context name=Add on the multi-lang corpus. The golden {"contexts":[],"count":0} is unchanged. This descriptor locks the name-only empty-result contract; the id= resolution path, errors[] reporting for unresolvable ids, and mode= fail-fast are covered by tests/parity/descriptors/mcp/get_context/basic.parity.json plus the HandlersFixture.GetContext* unit tests.

tiers._rationale_stable: Full envelope + body parity. Both binaries emit {"contexts":[],"count":0} for the name-only no-mode invocation, so result.content[].text is byte-stable.
tiers._rationale_ignore: Standard JSON-RPC envelope noise plus timing residue. result.content[].text is stable for this name-only invocation.
